mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading exceeding 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate recession fears and negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of observed months, or interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates capacity constraints and margin compression, forcing equity markets to price in lower profitability and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation index reading above 2.5 percent triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 65 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and profit margins, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 60-day returns falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth correlates with negative market returns.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index drop below 95 signals severe supply-side contraction and economic slowdown, triggering recession fears and negative equity repricing over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations when INDPRO falls below 95, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.
